picks.rationale: The Atlanta Braves, with ace Chris Sale (10-6, 2.06 ERA) starting, face the struggling Padres' pitcher Griffin Canning (1-8, 6.67 ERA), presenting a significant pitching mismatch. Atlanta has won two of the first three games in this series and is motivated to secure the series victory at home. The Braves are heavy favorites, and their strong offensive capabilities against a vulnerable starter should allow them to win by at least three runs, offering excellent value at +154.

picks.game: Tampa Bay Rays @ Toronto Blue Jays
picks.rationale: The Tampa Bay Rays have been dominant against the Blue Jays this season, holding an 8-1 head-to-head record and outscoring them 23-5 in the first three games of this series. Toronto's offense is in a severe slump, averaging a league-worst 3.94 runs per game and scoring two or fewer runs in 12 of their last 19 contests. Despite Shane Bieber's recent strong outing, the Rays' overall superior form and offensive struggles of the Blue Jays make Tampa Bay a confident moneyline pick.

The Arizona Diamondbacks are showing strong recent form, with starter Brandon Pfaadt (4-1, 4.58 ERA) boasting a 2.30 ERA across three July starts and the team holding a 4-0 record in his last four outings. Conversely, the St. Louis Cardinals have struggled, going 3-7 in Michael McGreevy's last ten starts and losing four of their last five games. Significant sharp reverse line movement has been observed, with the line shifting towards the Diamondbacks despite public money on the Cardinals, indicating professional bettors favor Arizona.

This matchup features a notable pitching advantage for the Detroit Tigers with Troy Melton (1.80 ERA) on the mound, while Kansas City's Randy Dobnak has also shown strong run-prevention capabilities. Comerica Park is generally not conducive to high-scoring games, further supporting an Under play. Despite public betting leaning towards the Over, the strong starting pitching and park factors suggest a lower-scoring affair, making the Under 8.5 at -112 a high-confidence pick.

With Atlanta's ace Chris Sale (2.06 ERA) starting, the Padres' scoring opportunities are expected to be limited. Although San Diego's starter Griffin Canning (6.67 ERA) has struggled, the market consensus for this game's total is consistently around 8 or 8.5 runs, making the verified line of 9.5 a full run higher than expected. This discrepancy, combined with Sale's dominance, provides significant value on the Under 9.5 at plus money.
